summary: Large sysdolphin/baselib particle-display decomp PR. It added C implementations for `particleSort` and the very large `psDispParticles`, tightened several psdisp globals/prototypes from `UNK_T` or scalar types to `HSD_Particle*`, `HSD_Fog*`, and `GXColor`, and expanded `HSD_psAppSRT` padding into float fields needed by the renderer. The PR body had no explanation and there were no human review comments, but the decomp.dev bot reported measurable progress: `particleSort` to 92.88%, `psDispParticles` to 39.03%, psdisp `extab` to 100%, and related `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and `extabindex` improvements.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: +1456/-7; added psdisp helper structs, typed externs, `particleSort`, and a large partial C body for `psDispParticles` covering particle sorting, GX/TEV state, fog, texture, matrix, point/line/trail rendering paths.

Header signature cleanup: added `baselib/forward.h` and changed `particleSort` from `UNK_T particleSort(s32, u8, UNK_T*, UNK_T*)` to `HSD_Particle* particleSort(s32, u8, HSD_Particle**, HSD_Particle**)`.
Struct refinement: replaced `HSD_psAppSRT` padding at 0x6C-0x98 with twelve explicit `f32` fields used by the new psdisp matrix/SRT calculations.

what_changed: `psdisp.c` gained baselib dependencies for camera, fog, lighting objects, matrices, psdisp TEV setup, state invalidation, and utilities, plus `<string.h>` and an explicit `__fabsf` mapping instead of `<math.h>`.
Added file-local helper types `psdisp_ParticleSortBucket`, `Point3d` as a `Vec3` alias, and `psdisp_ParticleSortCache`, with `STATIC_ASSERT` checks for bucket size 8 and cache size 0xF0.

Added or corrected typed externs for psdisp globals: `hsd_804D0908[146]` as particle-list heads, `psTexGroupArray_804D0B50`, `psNumCmdList_804D0C54`, `HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0` as the sort/cache buffer, `HSD_PSDisp_804D7908` as `HSD_Fog*`, and `HSD_PSDisp_804D7934` through `HSD_PSDisp_804D7944` as `GXColor`.

Implemented `particleSort(s32, u8, HSD_Particle**, HSD_Particle**)`, sorting linked `HSD_Particle` lists into 16 head/tail buckets based on `kind` bits and caching the split point in `HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0.xAC[arg0]` when the per-group stamp matches.

Implemented a large `psDispParticles(s32, u32)` body that iterates up to 16 particle groups from a bitmask, calls `particleSort`, filters tiny particles and `TexEdge` cases, initializes cached GX/TEV/fog/matrix state, and emits rendering setup/draw paths for particle display modes.

`psDispParticles` now directly drives many rendering-state operations visible in the diff: `psSetupTevInvalidState`, `psSetupTevCommon`, `psSetupTev`, `HSD_FogSet`, `GXSetBlendMode`, `GXSetAlphaCompare`, `GXSetChanCtrl`, `GXSetChanMatColor`, `GXSetChanAmbColor`, `GXSetTevColor`, `GXSetZMode`, `GXSetLineWidth`, `GXSetPointSize`, `GXSetCurrentMtx`, and final `HSD_StateInvalidate(-1)`.

`psstructs.h` changed `HSD_psAppSRT` from an opaque fill range after `ssx/ssy` into offset-named float fields `x6C` through `x98`, matching how the new psdisp code treats the struct as matrix/SRT scratch data.
smart_moves: Retyping globals and prototypes unlocked direct field access and reduced `UNK_T` usage in a renderer that heavily manipulates `HSD_Particle`, `HSD_Fog`, and packed `GXColor` values.

The new `psdisp_ParticleSortCache` documents the mixed global buffer layout well enough to support both particle-list split caching and matrix/projection scratch use, while `STATIC_ASSERT` guards prevent silent layout drift.

The `particleSort` implementation preserves linked-list bucket heads and tails and caches the already-sorted result by a stamp byte, matching the original optimization pattern instead of rebuilding on every call.

The large `psDispParticles` implementation keeps explicit stack-style locals, casts, repeated state-cache checks, and unrolled-looking control flow, which is appropriate for a partially matching decomp of a codegen-sensitive rendering function.
The PR fixed header-level types at the same time as the C body, so later callsites can use the real `HSD_Particle*` return and out-parameter types instead of propagating placeholders.
decomp_lessons: For baselib particle display, `HSD_psAppSRT` fields after `ssx/ssy` are not arbitrary padding: psdisp uses them as float matrix/SRT components after `PSMTXConcat` and scale extraction.

When a global is used both as a structured cache and raw float matrix/projection storage, model the known structured portion with size assertions and retain casts for the scratch use until the full layout is understood.
Large renderer functions can still be valuable when only partially matching: the bot report shows `psDispParticles` moved from 0.00% to 39.03% and `particleSort` from 0.00% to 92.88%.
GX state-machine decomp should preserve the original's state-caching pattern: compare cached globals/locals before calling expensive or order-sensitive `GXSet*`, TEV, fog, and matrix APIs.
Do not over-name uncertain math fields too early; offset names like `x6C` through `x98` are better than opaque padding and safer than invented semantics.
For codegen-sensitive functions, exact library symbol choice can matter; this PR explicitly maps `fabsf` to `__fabsf` rather than relying on `<math.h>`.
naming_conventions: File-private helper structs use a lowercase module prefix plus descriptive suffix, as in `psdisp_ParticleSortBucket` and `psdisp_ParticleSortCache`.
Known-address psdisp globals keep the project’s `HSD_PSDisp_804Dxxxx` naming convention when semantics are not fully established.

Some legacy/non-HSD globals remain address-based lowercase names, such as `hsd_804D0908`, while newly identified arrays use descriptive address-suffixed names like `psTexGroupArray_804D0B50` and `psNumCmdList_804D0C54`.

Public prototypes should be moved from `UNK_T` to real baselib forward types as soon as the evidence supports it, demonstrated by `particleSort` returning `HSD_Particle*` and taking `HSD_Particle**` out parameters.
Unknown struct members that are actively used should be split into offset-named typed fields, e.g. `HSD_psAppSRT.x6C` through `x98`, instead of retaining a fill array.
Matching-sensitive temporaries in very large functions may retain decompiler-style names such as `sp7A0`, `var_r16`, `temp_r3`, and `prev_kind` until a cleaner refactor is safe.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `extern float __fabsf(float); #define fabsf __fabsf` when the original object calls the runtime `__fabsf` symbol and `<math.h>` would not reproduce the same reference.
Use `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` for newly inferred helper layouts that alias existing data or stack storage.
Preserve unrolled bucket concatenation for `particleSort` buckets 0-7 and 8-15 rather than replacing it with a cleaner loop if the compiler shape is sensitive.
Packed color state is mirrored through `GXColor` globals and `s32` stack copies such as `*(s32*) &HSD_PSDisp_804D7934`, matching the way the original code likely handles 4-byte GX colors.

Reuse `HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0` as a `f32*` camera/projection cache in `psDispParticles` for `HSD_CObjGetViewingMtx`, `PSMTXInverse`, and `GXGetProjectionv`, while also modeling its particle-sort tail-cache field.
Keep explicit floating constants and casts in the huge draw function; the diff shows many `f64` stack constants and `(f32)` casts that are likely there to shape generated instructions.
Avoid redundant GX updates by checking cached blend, alpha, channel, color, fog, texture-wrap, z-mode, point/line size, and current-matrix state before issuing the corresponding API calls.

review_feedback.note: PR body was empty; no author explanation was available in the slice.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
GALE01 report: matched data 39.41% with +32 bytes; psdisp `extab` reached 100.00%.
Reported improvements: `psDispParticles` +5655 bytes, 0.00% to 39.03%; `particleSort` +973 bytes, 0.00% to 92.88%; `.sdata2` 8.70% to 82.00%; `extabindex` 0.00% to 91.67%; `.sdata` 0.00% to 50.00%.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `psDispParticles` and `particleSort` to see whether the remaining unmatched percentages were closed.
Investigate real semantics for `HSD_psAppSRT.x6C` through `x98` now that psdisp uses them as transform/matrix fields.
Identify concrete types for `psTexGroupArray_804D0B50` and `psNumCmdList_804D0C54` instead of leaving them as `void*`.
Clarify the full layout and name of `HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0`, which is used both as a particle-sort cache and as matrix/projection scratch storage.
Confirm the frame/stamp meaning of `HSD_PSDisp_804D6380[0]` and bytes in `HSD_PSDisp_8040C360` used by `particleSort` caching.
